text:
We start with 100 bulbs, all off.  
On round \(k\), we toggle bulbs at positions that are multiples of \(k\).  

A bulb ends up on if it is toggled an odd number of times.  
The number of times a bulb at position \(n\) is toggled equals the number of divisors of \(n\) (since each divisor \(k\) toggles it).  

So we need the count of numbers from 1 to 100 that have an odd number of divisors.  
A number has an odd number of divisors if and only if it is a perfect square.  

Perfect squares from 1 to 100:  
\(1^2 = 1\), \(2^2 = 4\), \(3^2 = 9\), \(4^2 = 16\), \(5^2 = 25\), \(6^2 = 36\), \(7^2 = 49\), \(8^2 = 64\), \(9^2 = 81\), \(10^2 = 100\).  

That’s 10 numbers.  

Thus, 10 bulbs are on.

Final integer: 10
